mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near the zero lower bound (0.15% as of April 2009), the discount rate applied to equity cash flows stays structurally compressed, mechanically inflating the present value of future earnings and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon as long as the rate remains near zero.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative or not significantly above unconditional base rate (65.5% positive) during any 6-month window while FEDFUNDS stays below 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the month-over-month increase in UNRATE decelerates relative to the prior month's increase (labor market deterioration slowing), this signals an approaching cyclical trough in layoffs, and equity markets price in the coming recovery, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: monthly UNRATE increase is smaller than the prior month's increase (e.g., +0.1pp vs +0.4pp)
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE rose from 8.9 to 9.0 April 2009 vintage vs larger prior increases
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE MoM increase decelerates, SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint stabilization where HOUST flattens near cyclical lows (505k units) while RSAFS shows sequential improvement indicates simultaneous bottoming of housing and consumer demand, a combination that historically precedes durable equity recoveries over a quarterly horizon; neither series alone is sufficient because housing can stabilize while consumption keeps falling or vice versa.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month change within +/-2% of flat AND RSAFS month-over-month change turns positive
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 505k March 2009 vintage near historic lows
failure_condition: When both conditions trigger jointly, SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in less than 55% of instances, or the joint signal underperforms either single-series signal in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime following a sharp industrial contraction, a deceleration in the year-over-year decline of INDPRO (the rate of contraction shrinking rather than industrial output itself rising) signals the trough of the manufacturing cycle and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the following month, but this relationship does not hold during an active supply shock when contraction is still accelerating.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO April 2009 vintage 97.0723 reflecting sharp contraction from prior levels
regime_hint: post-shock industrial recovery, not active acceleration of contraction
failure_condition: During periods of decelerating INDPRO YoY decline, SPX 20-day forward returns underperform the unconditional 65.5% positive base rate by more than 10 percentage points, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month shrinkage in the magnitude of PAYEMS declines (fewer jobs lost than the prior month) is a leading real-time indicator of improving labor demand that markets react to quickly, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as traders front-run the eventual payroll inflection.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS April 2009 vintage 132414 declining from prior peaks
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following a month with smaller PAYEMS decline than the prior month are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a rolling 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: If CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation falls below zero, signaling outright deflation, equity markets price in rising real debt burdens and a debt-deflation spiral risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month until deflationary pressure abates.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL April 2009 vintage 212.671 near flat/declining YoY
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months where CPI YoY is negative, or Rank IC is not significantly negative over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a recession-trough regime, HOUST reaching multi-decade lows (near 500k units) functions as a leading indicator because housing starts historically bottom several months before broad economic recovery and equity markets, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter once HOUST stabilizes at cyclical lows; this mechanism is regime-specific and does not apply during mid-cycle housing corrections.
candidate_trigger: HOUST at or below post-1960s historical lows and flattening for 2+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST 505k March 2009 vintage near record lows
failure_condition: Following HOUST stabilization at cyclical lows, SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of historical instances, or the signal fails to outperform an unconditional baseline in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is pinned near zero while UNRATE is still rising, markets interpret this as evidence of a liquidity trap where monetary policy has lost traction against deteriorating labor conditions, producing negative near-term S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as risk sentiment deteriorates despite accommodative policy; the two signals are jointly necessary since either alone is common and non-predictive.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.25% AND UNRATE rising for 3+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS 0.15% and UNRATE rising to 9.0% April 2009 vintage
failure_condition: When both conditions are jointly triggered, SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the joint interaction signal does not outperform either single-series baseline in walk-forward tests.
